bullets.text: Fed minutes: officials still expect at least one 2026 rate cut; war adds uncertainty but baseline is disinflation and cooling demand.
Fed proposes allowing banks/credit unions to use intermediaries to access FedNow, potentially accelerating instant-payments adoption and competition with private rails.
U.S.–Iran cease-fire remains fragile; disputes extend to Lebanon and alleged breaches, keeping regional escalation risk elevated.
Iran’s leverage via potential Strait of Hormuz tolls keeps a risk premium in oil; U.S. demands unrestricted passage.
Oil prices rebounded on cease-fire breach claims; analysts expect gasoline easing only if truce holds and shipping remains uninterrupted.
Trump threatens 50% tariffs on countries supplying weapons to Iran, raising broader trade-war and supply-chain risks beyond the Middle East conflict.
Equities: S&P 500 reclaimed key moving averages, signaling improved risk appetite; history implies better near-term returns but not guaranteed.
With an oil shock avoided, commentary shifts toward bonds benefiting from easing inflation fear and renewed rate-cut expectations.
Consumer demand softening: Constellation Brands pulls 2028 guidance as shoppers trade down to cheaper beer/wine amid uncertainty.
AI race intensifies: Meta launches a major new model; OpenAI plans IPO with retail share allocation, signaling capital-market maturation for frontier AI.
National-security tech scrutiny: appeals court keeps Pentagon 'supply chain risk' label on Anthropic, underscoring growing regulatory friction for AI vendors.
Physics: new results strongly constrain sterile neutrinos, narrowing beyond-Standard-Model options and reshaping dark-matter-inspired particle hypotheses.
Quantum computing: researchers improved tracking of data loss, a step toward practical error correction and more reliable quantum processors.
Right-to-repair: John Deere’s $99M settlement signals rising legal and economic pressure to open parts, manuals, and fixes across equipment ecosystems.
